summary: The diff implements the vast majority of the requested requirements across frontend and backend. All major sections (Hero, Services, Products, Pricing, Gallery, Contractor Zone, Quote Builder, Contact, Footer, Navigation, Floating WhatsApp, Admin Panel) are present. The Motoko backend actor implements product CRUD, business settings, and access control. A few specific acceptance criteria details need closer examination, particularly around the Admin Panel's product management persistence (uses localStorage rather than backend calls), and some minor items. Overall coverage is very high.

missing_requirements.requirement: Products can be added, edited, and deleted via the admin panel with changes persisted to the backend.
missing_requirements.reason: In AdminPanel.tsx, product add/remove operations use localStorage ('adminProducts') rather than calling backend CRUD endpoints. The backend has addProduct/editProduct/deleteProduct functions, but the admin panel does not call them — product management is purely client-side/localStorage.

hallucinated_features.description: Gallery includes three additional images not specified in the build request: project-electrical, project-paint-finish, and project-full-interior.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request specifies exactly six gallery images (kitchen, wardrobe, living, bedroom, office, bathroom). The diff adds three extra gallery items (project-electrical.dim_800x600.png, project-paint-finish.dim_800x600.png, project-full-interior.dim_800x600.png) that were not requested.

hallucinated_features.description: User profile management (UserProfile type, getCallerUserProfile, getUserProfile, saveCallerUserProfile) is implemented in the backend actor.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request does not mention any user profile functionality. The backend adds UserProfile data model and three profile-related query/update functions that were never requested.
